channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire appears to hold and Lebanese begin streaming south to their homes
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah appeared to be holding on Wednesday, as residents in cars heaped with belongings streamed back toward southern Lebanon despite warnings from the Israeli and Lebanese military that they stay away.
item.dc:creator: Kareem Chehayeb And Tia Goldenberg, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pakistan ends lockdown of its capital after Imran Khan supporters are dispersed by police
item.description: Authorities have reopened roads linking Pakistan’s capital with the rest of the country, ending a four-day lockdown, after using tear gas and firing into the air to disperse supporters of imprisoned former Prime Minister Imran Khan who demanded his release from prison.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Namibia votes for president and the chance for its first female leader
item.description: WINDHOEK, Namibia (AP) — A 72-year-old woman who joined Namibia's independence movement in the 1970s is a strong contender to become its first female leader as the country votes in a presidential election on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Worst November snowstorm in half century hits Seoul and grounds hundreds of airplane flights
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The most severe November snowstorm to hit South Korea's capital in more than a half century blanketed the capital on Wednesday, grounding hundreds of airplane flights and disrupting commuter traffic.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australian father of teen sextortion victim backs banning young children from social media
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Wayne Holdsworth became an advocate for banning Australian children younger than 16 from social media because his son took his own life after falling victim to an online sextortion scam.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Texas Democrat says Trump's tariffs 'will definitely get Mexico to the table' to solve immigration, fentanyl problems
item.description: Rep. Henry Cuellar (D-Texas) said Tuesday that President-elect Trump’s tariffs “will definitely get Mexico to the table” to solve immigration and fentanyl problems.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: G7 ministers throw support behind Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire but make no mention of ICC warrant
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — Foreign ministers from leading industrialized countries threw their strong support Tuesday behind an immediate ceasefire between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah but sidestepped the question of whether to enforce an international arrest warrant for Israel’s leade
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorist' list captured in UK after 20 years
item.description: Daniel Andreas San Diego, a fugitive on the FBI’s “Most Wanted Terrorist” list, was captured in the United Kingdom on Monday after more than 20 years. San Diego, 46, wanted for his alleged involvement in two bombings in Northern California in the 2000s, was arrested in Wales, the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Unidentified drones spotted over US military sites in UK for second week
item.description: The Pentagon is “actively” watching for drones over U.S. Air Force locations in the United Kingdom, following a series of unexplained sightings over the past two weeks. The sightings, which took place over three U.S. air bases last week between Nov.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Biden to make new push for ceasefire in Gaza after Israel-Hezbollah deal
item.description: President Biden on Tuesday said the U.S. will make a new push for a ceasefire in Gaza in the coming days, after Israel and Hezbollah agreed to pause their fighting in and around the border with Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Russian migrants being denied access to asylum process: attorney
item.description: "They are being subjected to a blanket policy that they are applying to Russian asylum seekers," he said.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Trudeau says he had 'good call' with Trump amid tariff threats
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau said Tuesday he had a “good call” with President-elect Trump following the incoming U.S. leader’s pronouncement that he would impose a 25 percent tariff on Canadian goods when he takes office.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Israel, Lebanon's Hezbollah agree to a ceasefire to end nearly 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israel approved a United States-brokered ceasefire agreement with Lebanon's Hezbollah on Tuesday, setting the stage for an end to nearly 14 months of fighting linked to the ongoing war in the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New subway system in Greece puts archaeological finds discovered during construction on display for passengers
item.description: A new subway system is on its way to being showcased to passengers in Thessaloniki, Greece. The project unveiled thousands of ancient finds through excavations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire to start Wednesday morning
item.description: The Israeli and U.S. governments announced a ceasefire agreement with Hezbollah in Lebanon that will begin Wednesday morning at 4 a.m. local time. “I have good news from the Middle East,” President Biden said in remarks from the White House Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Germany’s Merkel: Trump ‘does not believe in any win-win situation,’ making collaboration difficult
item.description: Former German Chancellor Angela Merkel argued President-elect Trump does not believe in “any win-win situation,” and it makes international collaboration difficult. Merkel, who served as chancellor from 2005-21, has seen U.S. presidents come and go.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Blinken to appear at Afghanistan withdrawal hearing, top Republican says
item.description: Rep. Michael McCaul (R-Texas), the outgoing chair of the House Foreign Affairs Committee, said Secretary of State Antony Blinken is expected to testify before the committee about the Biden administration's withdrawal of troops from Afghanistan.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine says Russian attack sets a new record for the number of drones used
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia launched 188 drones against most regions of Ukraine in a nighttime blitz, the Ukrainian air force said Tuesday, describing it as a record number of drones deployed in a single attack.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexican cattle imports suspended after New World screwworm detected
item.description: The United States Department of Agriculture’s Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service (APHIS) announced that a New World screwworm (NWS) was detected in Mexico on Friday, causing the agency to pause animal imports from the country. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: A violent showdown in Pakistan's capital pits the government against Imran Khan supporters
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Thousands of people are in the heart of Pakistan’s capital to demand the release of imprisoned former premier Imran Khan. He has been behind bars since August 2023 on charges that he and his supporters say are politically motivated.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada braces for surge of immigrants hoping to move from US
item.description: Canadian officials will limit how many new permanent residents it allows amid an expected surge at the northern border.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, The Hill

item.title: Philippine president and vice president clash in a feud that's testing an Asian democracy
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A major political storm set off by a bitter feud between the Philippine president and the vice president is testing an Asian democracy that has long been troubled by rowdy politics, poor law enforcement and bareface impunity.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian forces capture former British soldier fighting for Ukraine in Kursk: report
item.description: A former British Army soldier was reportedly captured by Russian forces while fighting alongside Ukrainian soldiers in the Kursk region of the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pledges Mexico, Canada, China tariffs over border, fentanyl
item.description: "Thousands of people are pouring through Mexico and Canada, bringing Crime and Drugs at levels never seen before," Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Germany's Merkel recalls Putin's 'power games' and contrasting US presidents in her memoirs
item.description: Former German Chancellor Angela Merkel recalls Vladimir Putin’s “power games” over the years, remembers contrasting meetings with Barack Obama and Donald Trump and says she asked herself whether she could have done more to prevent Brexit.
item.dc:creator: Geir Moulson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Two senators urge FIFA not to pick Saudi Arabia as 2034 host
item.description: Democrats Ron Wyden of Oregon and Dick Durbin of Illinois have told FIFA president Gianni Infantino in a letter “we urge you to seek out a host country with a record of upholding human rights.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah fires barrage of rockets into Israel after IDF targets Hezbollah command centers in Beirut
item.description: Hezbollah on Sunday fired missiles and other projectiles into Israel in retaliation for to deadly IDF strikes on the militant group’s command centers in Beirut.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Four men convicted in Spain in connection to 2021 hate crime murder
item.description: Four men were sentenced for their involvement in the homophobic murder of 24-year-old Samuel Luiz, who died at a hospital in July 2021 after being attacked for allegedly being gay.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shootout near Israeli Embassy in Jordan leaves 3 police officers injured, gunman dead
item.description: Police in Jordan shot and killed a gunman who wounded three police officers near the Israeli Embassy on Sunday morning, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli strikes in central Beirut kill at least 20 as diplomats push for a cease-fire
item.description: BEIRUT, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli airstrikes Saturday in central Beirut killed at least 20 people, officials said, as the once-rare attacks on the heart of Lebanon's capital continued without warning while diplomats scrambled to broker a cease-fire.
item.dc:creator: ABBY SEWELL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: A look inside the most expensive house in the world — the home of the UK’s monarch
item.description: Buckingham Palace in London is worth about $4.9 billion and has about 775 rooms, including royal and guest rooms, offices and bathrooms.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers make last-ditch effort to safeguard US support
item.description: Pro-Ukraine lawmakers in both parties are working to get key provisions surrounding U.S. support for Kyiv passed into law by January, an effort to safeguard assistance that is under threat in a transition to a Trump administration and full GOP control of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Catherine Herridge wants Trump 'sit-down' on bipartisan press bill
item.description: “This case involves the First Amendment and the right of reporters in the future,” Catherine Herridge said.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu ‘in very bad company’ with ICC indictment: William Cohen
item.description: The ICC issued arrest warrants for Netanyahu and a former defense minister on war crimes allegations.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Top war-crimes court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu and others in Israel-Hamas fighting
item.description: THE HAGUE (AP) — The world’s top war-crimes court issued arrest warrants Thursday for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and Hamas’ military chief, accusing them of crimes against humanity in connection with the 13-month war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US vetoes UN resolution demanding a cease-fire in Gaza because there's no link to a hostage release
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Wednesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution demanding an immediate cease-fire in the war in Gaza because it was not linked to an immediate release of hostages taken captive by Hamas militants in Israel in October 2023.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, WGN 9
